mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive return rate in top-quartile INDPRO growth months is less than 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation captured by CPIAUCSL erodes real corporate margins and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns when inflation exceeds a critical threshold.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50 percent of triggered high-inflation months, or the mean forward return during triggers is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in the labor market, simultaneous contraction in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE), signals falling aggregate income and weakening consumption, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction score with 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future earnings, reducing equity present value and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 realizes positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent during identified tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer expenditure, which precedes downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45 percent of months with declining RSAFS, or the hit rate of the signal is not statistically different from the baseline negative rate (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals excess labor supply and weakening household income growth, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50 percent of triggered rising-unemployment episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strengthening residential investment and broader credit availability, which boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST and 60-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) captures aggregate output expansion; positive growth at the vintage release date predicts upward revisions in corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change is greater than zero
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or the mean return during positive growth quarters does not exceed the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).
